zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Webb於1560年出生在英國維爾爾哥吉拉 卡寧斯時，她的父親Sir Thomas Webb15歲，母親Margaret19歲。她於1580年5月7日與Richard Hitchcock在英國英格蘭維爾爾特洛布利其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1621年12月2日在英國維爾爾享年61歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾。

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Webb was born in 1560, in All Cannings, Wiltshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas Webb, was 15 and her mother, Margaret, was 19. She married Richard Hitchcock on 7 May 1580, in Trowbridge, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 2 December 1621, in Wiltshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Webb est née en 1560, à All Cannings, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Webb, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Hitchcock le 7 mai 1580, à Trowbridge, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 2 décembre 1621, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Wiltshire, Angleterre.

Lei sposato Richard Hitchcock il 7 maggio 1580, nel Trowbridge, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. ko.lifeSummary: Maud Webb(이)가 1560년 영국 윌츠히르 앨 캔잉스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Webb(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1580년 5월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 트로우브리지에서 Richard Hitchcock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1621년 12월 2일 영국 윌츠히르에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Webb родилась в 1560 г. в местности алл каннингс, уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Webb было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Hitchcock 7 мая 1580 г. в местности троубридж, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 2 декабря 1621 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности уилцхир, Англия.
